title: Binary Tree Right Side View
prompt:
Given the root of a binary tree, imagine standing on the right side of it. Return the values of the nodes you can see from top to bottom.

If the tree is empty, return an empty array.
constraintQuestions: What should we return for an empty tree? ([]).
Should we return values from top to bottom? (Yes.)
Are there duplicate values? (Possibly, but order still matters.)
Can we solve with BFS (last node each level) or DFS (right-first)?
Do we only return one value per level? (Yes.)

walkthrough.title: BFS (last value per level)
walkthrough.body:
Use a queue to traverse level by level.
For each level, process all nodes and record the last node's value.
Push that value into the result.

codeSnippets.title: JavaScript
C#

walkthrough.title: DFS (right-first preorder)
walkthrough.body:
Traverse right-first so the first time you reach a level, it's the rightmost node.
If level === result.length, push the node value.
Then recurse into right child, then left child.

codeSnippets.title: JavaScript
C#

walkthrough.title: Complexity
walkthrough.body:
Time: O(n) because each node is visited once.
Space: O(w) for BFS queue or O(h) for DFS recursion.
